articles.title: My Husband
articles.author: Mary C. Martineau
articles.complete_text:
His love is like a great warm coat,
That keeps out the wintry weather;
It folds me round with it's comfort dear,
Though the storms of life may gather.
Though my heart may weep, it still is warm
And trustingly plans the morrow,
For the great coat of his love protects,
And heals my every sorrow.

His love is like the sunshine warm
And the best of my nature keeps blooming,
For it's easy to smile and be sweet and true
When the sun of your life keeps wooing.
His smile brings back a smile in return,
His praise crushes dead all repining
And my soul climbs up to new heights every day
To keep my sun ever shining.
articles.notes: This is a complete standalone poem with clear title and author attribution, appearing in the editorial section of the magazine

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of legitimate miscellaneous material including: table of contents for the February 1927 issue, masthead/publication information, extensive advertisements for various products and services (jewelry, travel, garments, food products, etc.), editorial notes about contest winners and upcoming articles, and a correction notice. This is all appropriate misc content that should not be extracted as standalone articles.
